title: The Brew Shop - Weekly Email Update
contentHtml:
Hey Brew Shop Fam!
Here's an update of what's happening this week - from new beer drops, wine reviews, our current tap list, to upcoming tastings, and more. 

Join us this Friday from 5-7 PM as we welcome Alek from Impero Wine for our free tasting! Scroll down to see our full tasting line-up for July.

🥳 Pure Project is here!!!!
• Sunsip Non-Alcoholic Blonde
• I Choose Yuzu Japanese Rice Lager with Yuzu
• Rain German Pilsner
• Rewild West Coast Pale Ale with Centennial & Citra
• Pure West West Coast IPA with Nelson Sauvin, Citra, Strata & Simcoe
• Super Beta Hazy IPA with Nelson Sauvin, Motueka, Southern Cross & Mosaic Cryo
• Diamond Dust Hazy IPA with Cashmere & Mosaic
• Beyond the Shore West Coast IPA with Simcoe, Krush & Simcoe Cryo - collab with MadeWest
• Treasure Reef Hazy DIPA with Strata, Citra Cryo & Nelson Abstrax

👊 Bierstadt Lagerhaus returns!
• The Slow Pour Pils
• Helles
• Dunkel
• Italian Pils

🤤 Mortalis keeping it decadent
• Hydra | Banana + Strawberry + Pineapple Smoothie Sour
• Hydra | Blackberry + Pineapple + Coconut Smoothie Sour
• Hydra | Peach + Strawberry + Mango + Ice Cream Smoothie Sour
• Hera Pineapple DIPA with Citra, Columbus, Centennial & Galaxy blended with pineapple puree
• Gemini Bomb Pop Sour
• Zombie Gods and Goddesses DIPA with Citra & Cryo Citra (collab with Beer Zombies)

🏔️ Straight from the mountains with Outer Range
• In The Steep Hazy IPA with Citra
• Forest Bathing IPA with Citra & Simcoe
• Blocks of Light Hazy IPA with Galaxy & Mosaic
• Touch Grass West Coast IPA with Simcoe Cryo, Simcoe Quantum, El Dorado & Cascade Cryo
• Lo and Behold Hazy IPA with Barbe Rouge & Mosaic
• Water Colors Hazy IPA with Citra, Simcoe & Columbus

👋 From our friends at Lost Generation
• Tangerine Mango Crush Sour
• Grave Shift Dark Lager
• Babylon Revisited West Coast IPA with Nelson Sauvin, Cashmere & Exp-2B

✨Oh hey, Divine Barrel!
• What Glitters is Gold German Hefe
• 100 Months West Coast IPA with Centennial
• Moving to Montana West Coast IPA with Citra, Simcoe & Strata

🔪 Hop Butcher slaying all the hops
• Emits Showers of Sparks DIPA with Mosaic, Sabro & Galaxy
• Wobby Sobby DIPA with Nectaron, Mosaic, Mosaic Cryo, Nelson Sauvin, Thora & Nelson Sauvin Amplifire Oil
• The Day We Floated DIPA with Nelson, Citra, Citra Cryo, Tangier, Riwaka & Riwaka Sub Zero Hop Kief
• Quarter Stick DIPA with Motueka, Citra, Sabro, Vic Secret & NZ Cascade Subzero Hop Kief
• Greener Moss Triple IPA with Citra & Simcoe

🍭 It’s all lollipops and chaos with Deciduous
• Lollipop Forest PB&J Sour
• By Myself Nelson Hazy IPA with Nelson
• Created Chaos DDH DIPA with Idaho Gem, Krush & BRU-1

Fun from Triple Crossing!

• Baby Falcon Session IPA

• Clever Girl IPA with Citra & Mosaic

• Nectar & Knife Hazy DIPA with Mosaic & Simcoe

🍅 New Other Half x 5!
• Blue Cheese DIPA with Nectaron, Mosaic, Motueka & Nelson Sauvin
• Fresh Tag Imperial IPA with Citra, Citra Cryo, Mosaic, Mosaic Cryo & Columbus Cryo
• Header Juice Hazy IPA with Citra, Citra Lupulin, Anchovy & Peacharine
• Spinach IPA with Citra, Mosaic, El Dorado & Nelson Sauvin
• Tomato Factory DIPA with Citra, Vic Secret, Riwaka and Sabro

💧 Just dropping
• Commonwealth Future Classic DIPA with Strata, Citra & Hallertau Blanc
• Japas Cervejaria Shibui Kolsch
• Drowned Lands Lush Yield IPA with Citra, Azacca & Simcoe

The latest from The Veil

• For Love American Light Lager

• Cat Nap Hazy IPA with Mosaic, Nelson, Galaxy & Citra

• Doom Volume Hazy IPA with Citra, Strata, Galaxy & Citra Incognito

Ardent back in stock!

• Atlantico Mexican Lager

• Blackberry Lime Gose

• Pineapple Mango Gose

Cheers to

• Aslin Green Grass West Coast IPA

• Vasen Fox Ridge Saison

• Shop fav Von Trapp Oktoberfest is back for the season!

Forest & Main Weathervane West Coast IPA

Wild East Turnstyle Nopal Margarita Sour

New wines in the door!

Poggio Costa Prosecco Rose:Poggio Costa means "The Hill by the Sea" referring to the hillside ranges along the northern Adriatic coast of Italy. If you can believe it, Prosecco Rosé is a relatively new official category only receiving its DOC designation in 2020. Delicate and dry, Glera and Pinot Nero make the perfect duo - Glera with the floral charm and Pinot Nero the color and red berry bursts. Sustainable.

Colutta Pinot Grigio Ramato: Ramato is a historic style of skin contact Pinot Grigio that has ridden the wave of orange wine back into popularity of its own. While orange wine can be made from any white grape, Ramato is specifically and only Pinot Grigio. (Fun fact, while Pinot Grigio is a "white wine", its grapes actually do not have a white skin - they're grayish-pink / purple!) If you're new to Ramato, Colutta is a great place to start - fresh and bright with notes of peach, apple, and citrus rind plus balanced acidity and gentle, dusty tannins.

Southwest Mountain Cabernet Franc: A chillable red Virginia Cab Franc? Absolutely. Get ready for VA Cab Franc like you've never seen it before. Crunchy red fruit and brambly dark fruit sit inside an incredibly light-on-its-feet body. Floral notes and fine tannins plus a hint of candied texture that makes an incredibly complex wine feel simple and accessible. And give that puppy a swirl and a sniff - this is one of the most intoxicating smelling wines in the shop.

Mary Taylor Cotes de Gascogne Rouge: Mary Taylor believes that wine should tell you where it's from and who made it, and that price should reflect the Old World view of wine as an everyday luxury, not a special occasion. We love Mary's philosophy and her wines. While the Côtes de Gascogne is overwhelmingly white wine country — reds make up less than 10% of production — Côtes de Gascogne Rouge is a regional red worth knowing. An unoaked blend of native but familiar varietals with dark berry fruit, lush roundness and grippy tannins. Falling in that sweet middle spot somewhere between a picnic wine and a dinner wine. Sustainable.

 

Poggio Costa Prosecco Rose:Poggio Costa means "The Hill by the Sea" referring to the hillside ranges along the northern Adriatic coast of Italy. If you can believe it, Prosecco Rosé is a relatively new official category only receiving its DOC designation in 2020. Delicate and dry, Glera and Pinot Nero make the perfect duo - Glera with the floral charm and Pinot Nero the color and red berry bursts. Sustainable.

Colutta Pinot Grigio Ramato: Ramato is a historic style of skin contact Pinot Grigio that has ridden the wave of orange wine back into popularity of its own. While orange wine can be made from any white grape, Ramato is specifically and only Pinot Grigio. (Fun fact, while Pinot Grigio is a "white wine", its grapes actually do not have a white skin - they're grayish-pink / purple!) If you're new to Ramato, Colutta is a great place to start - fresh and bright with notes of peach, apple, and citrus rind plus balanced acidity and gentle, dusty tannins.

Southwest Mountain Cabernet Franc: A chillable red Virginia Cab Franc? Absolutely. Get ready for VA Cab Franc like you've never seen it before. Crunchy red fruit and brambly dark fruit sit inside an incredibly light-on-its-feet body. Floral notes and fine tannins plus a hint of candied texture that makes an incredibly complex wine feel simple and accessible. And give that puppy a swirl and a sniff - this is one of the most intoxicating smelling wines in the shop.

Mary Taylor Cotes de Gascogne Rouge: Mary Taylor believes that wine should tell you where it's from and who made it, and that price should reflect the Old World view of wine as an everyday luxury, not a special occasion. We love Mary's philosophy and her wines. While the Côtes de Gascogne is overwhelmingly white wine country — reds make up less than 10% of production — Côtes de Gascogne Rouge is a regional red worth knowing. An unoaked blend of native but familiar varietals with dark berry fruit, lush roundness and grippy tannins. Falling in that sweet middle spot somewhere between a picnic wine and a dinner wine. Sustainable.

Pillitteri Estate Vidal Icewine is a vibrant and welcome addition to the shop. A sip of this wine can make these hot summer days a bit sweeter and brighter. If icewine is new to you, it is produced from grapes left to naturally freeze on the vine in the coldest months of winter. This natural freeze allows for the concentration of sugars and flavors into a delightful aromatic dessert wine. When popping this dessert wine, let the aromas capture your senses with wildflower honey, stone fruit, and soft undertones of pineapple and orange peel. Whether you are eating a rich piece of chocolate cake or a charcuterie board, this icewine is surely a perfect pairing, showcasing naturally vibrant acidity and a fresh, balancing sweetness for any celebration or picnic.

This week’s writeup is brought to you by Sam B.

July Tasting Events coming your way!

Thanks for your business! See you soon, Beth, Julie & Aidan and the entire Brew Crew
